# Muscle Booster - Trainingsplan · Fitness

Generated: Jul 22, 2026, 11:11 AM

## **Contents** {#toc}

- [Bugs and stability](#theme-bugs_stability)
- [Pricing and paywall](#theme-pricing_paywall)


## **Bugs and stability** {#theme-bugs_stability}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      2 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jul 7, 2026 - Jul 12, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _effednn007_, Jul 7, 2026, 2/5: "I’ve used this app for nearly 2 years and had great results with the workouts. Seems like if you have a technical problem you’re on your own. The app just stopped working one day. Probably from the latest update. Reaching out to support for several days just got me automated emails pointing me to a FAQ. Following the guidance in the FAQ made the issue even worse. And now all my historical data is gone and I am unable to access my account. Still no contact from an actual human. I’d love to get the app working again, even if I have to start from scratch, but so far I’m not encouraged."
            2. _C95746_, Jul 12, 2026, 3/5: "I was not impressed by the app. It was too easy and did not give you clear ways to level up. There were several exercise exercises for women on their stomach, which is also very difficult if you have a bad back. The alternatives were limited and only included other exercises on your stomach. The app was also glitchy and that it was comparing a BMI above my recorded input against man’s BMI. This was billed as military calisthenics for women however, once you get in the app, there’s absolutely no tie to that at all. There’s no music or energy which also makes it a bit boring."

## **Pricing and paywall** {#theme-pricing_paywall}

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      2 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Mar 2, 2026 - Jul 14, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _B Setzer_, Jul 14, 2026, 1/5: "Signed up for trial and was charged 4 times for $49.99. When I tried to get it fixed, they cancelled my trial. Very disappointed!"
            2. _ChuckIAM_, Jun 29, 2026, 3/5: "This app avoids the Apple subscription service and bills you directly through a website. We canceled this and we’re still billed for a month service. We were told that we were. ‘Eligible’ for a refund even though we canceled. It’s clear why they are avoiding Apple subscription service. My bank even flagged this as fraudulent. I would consider strongly before subscribing to this service."
            3. _Cr3epy_, May 15, 2026, 3/5: "Absolute overpriced App - there are ton of other subscriptions with better quality or equivalent content. Dont buy here and fall into the price trap… Abo Trap even when you cancel through the app update: Payment Refunded"
